Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.2: You can't cast spells or concentrate on spells. Your ability to speak is reduced to short, basic, guttural responses.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.3: While transformed, you can use weapons and equipment as normal, unless specified elsewhere.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.4: You gain a Claw or Bite attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. These attacks deal {@dice 1d8} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.5: After taking an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you may take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later in that turn to attack once with either your Claw or Bite.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.3: Your hybrid wolf form lasts 1 hour per Transformation Stage. If you entered your hybrid form voluntarily, you can revert to your normal form by using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn. ({@filter Flaws listed below|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ly:tf|miscellaneous=} may override this rule.)
Hybrid Wolf Form.name: Hybrid Wolf Form
Illusionary Cloak.entries.0: You can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to wrap yourself in an illusionary cloak for up to 1 hour. During this time, you can make yourself look like another creature of your general size and humanoid shape. This illusion ends when you use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to end it, or if you die or gain the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.
Illusionary Cloak.entries.1: A creature can pierce the illusion by taking the {@action Study|XPHB} action and succeeding on a DC 20 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check, thus seeing your true form.
Illusionary Cloak.name: Illusionary Cloak
Impromptu Artisan.entries.0: You've never known the luxury of always having the  gear you need, but you have more than learned to make do. If you possess {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with which you have proficiency, and if you have access to appropriate raw materials and any additional necessary equipment (as the GM determines), you can use a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} to craft any one nonmagical item worth 10 GP or less, including:
Impromptu Artisan.entries.1.items.2: A unique item that performs a simple function approved by the GM
Impromptu Artisan.entries.2: The gear you create is workable but not high quality, and can't be sold except as the GM determines.
Impromptu Artisan.entries.3.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can use Impromptu Artisan during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, during which you craft one nonmagical item worth 50 GP or less.
Impromptu Artisan.entries.3.name: Master Artisan
Impromptu Artisan.name: Impromptu Artisan
Improved Fanged Bite.entries.0: As an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make 2 {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attacks. Additionally, you can make a {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} if you don't make a {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action on the same turn.
Improved Fanged Bite.name: Improved Fanged Bite
Improved Pact Weapon.entries.0: You can use any weapon you summon with your Pact of the Blade feature as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells.
Improved Pact Weapon.entries.1: In addition, the weapon gains a +1 bonus to its attack and damage rolls, unless it is a magic weapon that already has a bonus to those rolls.
Improved Pact Weapon.entries.2: Finally, the weapon you conjure can be a {@item shortbow|phb}, {@item longbow|phb}, {@item light crossbow|phb}, or {@item heavy crossbow|phb}.
Improved Pact Weapon.name: Improved Pact Weapon
Improviser.entries.0: When needs demand, you get the job done better than most. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose one skill or tool that you don't have proficiency with. You have proficiency in that skill or with that tool for 1 hour. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Improviser.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks you make using the skill or tool you select with Improviser. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Improviser.entries.1.name: Expert Improviser
Improviser.name: Improviser
Inborn Perception.entries.0: The best way to avoid danger is to make sure you're the first person to notice it. You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception|XPHB} skill.
Inborn Perception.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Perception checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Inborn Perception.entries.1.name: Piercing Perception
Inborn Perception.name: Inborn Perception
Incorporeal Movement.entries.0: You are able to loosen the binds that tie you to the material world to move through solid objects and creatures. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can make yourself incorporeal until the start of your next turn. While in this form, you gain the following benefits:
Incorporeal Movement.entries.1.items.0: You can move through solid objects at your full {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You take {@damage 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a solid object and are moved to the nearest open space.
Incorporeal Movement.entries.1.items.1: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage types except Force damage.
Incorporeal Movement.entries.2: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Incorporeal Movement.name: Incorporeal Movement
Infernal Smite.entries.0: Once on each of your turns, you can choose one creature you have just damaged with a weapon attack, an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or a cantrip. Deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Acid, Cold, or Fire damage to that creature, using the same damage type you chose for {@optfeature Fiendish Soul|GrimHollowPG24}. You can add this damage a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage, but no more than once per damage roll. You regain all uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Infernal Smite.entries.1: At higher Transformation Stages, you do an additional {@damage 1d6} of damage per Transformation Stage, for a total of {@dice 2d6} at Stage 2, {@dice 3d6} at Stage 3, and {@dice 4d6} at Stage 4.
Infernal Smite.name: Infernal Smite
Infernal Summons.entries.0: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can tear open a portal to the Netherworld and summon up to four {@filter Fiends of CR 4|bestiary|source=|Challenge Rating=[&0;&4]|type=fiend} or lower within 30 feet of you. The Fiends remain for 1 minute (or less, if the GM decides what Fiends answer the summons).
Infernal Summons.entries.1: The summoned Fiends are {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your companions. They act directly after you in the initiative order. They obey any verbal commands that you issue to them (no action required by you). If you don't issue any commands to them, they use the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action on their turn and take no other movement, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Bonus Actions}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} unless it is to protect themselves.
Infernal Summons.name: Infernal Summons
Infused (Dragon, Elemental, Ooze).entrie: Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. For 10 minutes, whenever you hit with an attack, you can cause it to deal the chosen type rather than its normal damage type.
Infused (Dragon, Elemental, Ooze).name: Infused (Dragon, Elemental, Ooze)
Inhuman Reflexes.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.
Inhuman Reflexes.name: Inhuman Reflexes
Instinctive Stealth.entries.0: When trouble comes for you, you excel at making sure it can't find you. You have proficiency in the {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skill.
Instinctive Stealth.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Stealth|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Instinctive Stealth.entries.1.name: Calculated Disappearance
Instinctive Stealth.name: Instinctive Stealth
Instrumentalist.entries.0: In the quieter moments, music can help you forget the horrors you've seen. You have proficiency with {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB|two instruments} of your choice.
Instrumentalist.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain proficiency with two new instruments each time.
Instrumentalist.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made using any instrument. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Instrumentalist.entries.1.name: Virtuoso
Instrumentalist.name: Instrumentalist
Intrinsic Orientation.entries.0: A single misstep can lead to ruin, but your instincts for direction keep you from going astray. You always know which way is north, and you can reckon a cardinal direction of the GM's determination while on other planes. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made to avoid becoming lost, to navigate, or to track.
If you take this trait twice, when you fail an ability check made to avoid becoming lost, to navigate, or to track, you can choose to succeed instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Intrinsic Orientation.entries.1.name: Expert Orientation
Intrinsic Orientation.name: Intrinsic Orientation
Intuitive Acrobat.entries.0: Staying loose and limber means being able to get out of even the tightest spots when your life is on the line. You have proficiency in the {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} skill.
Intuitive Acrobat.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Intuitive Acrobat.entries.1.name: Stunt Expert
Intuitive Acrobat.name: Intuitive Acrobat
Inured to the Elements.entries.0: Even beneath scorching sun and in freezing cold, you hold yourself strong. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws made to resist the effects of extreme cold or extreme heat.
Inured to the Elements.entries.1.entries: If you take this trait twice, you automatically succeed on Constitution saving throws to resist the effects of extreme cold or extreme heat.
Inured to the Elements.entries.1.name: Immune to the Elements
Inured to the Elements.name: Inured to the Elements
Investment of the Chain Master.entries.0: When you cast {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB}, you infuse the summoned familiar with a measure of your eldritch power, granting the creature the following benefits.
Investment of the Chain Master.entries.1: The familiar gains either a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} or a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} (your choice) of 40 feet.
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can command the familiar to take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action.
Whenever the familiar deals Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can make it deal Necrotic or Radiant damage instead.
If the familiar forces a creature to make a saving throw, it uses your spell save DC.
When the familiar takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to grant it {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} against that damage.
Investment of the Chain Master.name: Investment of the Chain Master
Invisibility (Fey, Fiend, Undead).entrie: For 10 minutes, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition. This effect ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), or you attack or cast a spell.
Invisibility (Fey, Fiend, Undead).name: Invisibility (Fey, Fiend, Undead)
Iron Pelt.entries.0: While in hybrid form, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Magical and silvered weapons ignore this {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.
Iron Pelt.name: Iron Pelt
Iron Sensitivity.entries.0: Your body and soul connect back to the Realm of Faerie, and iron and steel weapons forged of the mortal realm cause you pain. After each {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the first time you are damaged by an iron or steel weapon, you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.
Iron Sensitivity.name: Iron Sensitivity
Ironmind Injection.entries.0: For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.
Ironmind Injection.name: Ironmind Injection
Irrepressible Sight.entries.0: Any foe you can see is a foe you can take down—so you make sure nothing prevents you from seeing. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against having the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition.
Irrepressible Sight.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against having the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Irrepressible Sight.entries.1.name: Resolute Sight
Irrepressible Sight.name: Irrepressible Sight
Keen Survivor.entries.0: The wilds of Etharis have claimed many who lack the skill to navigate them. You have proficiency in the {@skill Survival|XPHB} skill.
Keen Survivor.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Survival|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Keen Survivor.entries.1.name: Determined Survivor
Keen Survivor.name: Keen Survivor
Kindred Form.entries.0: You gain the ability to transform into the animal form representative of your Lycanthropy type, known as your Kindred Form. Use the stat block of the creature your lycanthropy corresponds with and follow the rules of the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell:
Kindred Form.entries.1.items.0.name: Werebear:
Kindred Form.entries.1.items.1.name: Wererat:
Kindred Form.entries.1.items.2.name: Werewolf:
Kindred Form.entries.2: You are indistinguishable from other creatures of that type, and you can communicate with those creatures. You cannot speak, but you can understand languages you know. All equipment you wear and carry falls off you when transforming.
Kindred Form.name: Kindred Form
Lance of Lethargy.entries.0: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with your {@spell eldritch blast}, you can reduce that creature's speed by 10 feet until the end of your next turn.
Lance of Lethargy.name: Lance of Lethargy
Larger Target.entries.0: Foes that outsize you quickly learn to fear your wrath. If you hit a creature that is one size larger than you, you can choose to deal extra damage to the creature equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Larger Target.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, Larger Target applies to creatures of any size larger than you.
Larger Target.entries.1.name: Even Larger
Larger Target.name: Larger Target
Legion of Slime.entries.0: When you manifest your Ooze Form, it lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Legion of Slime.entries.1: While you are manifesting your Ooze Form and you are hit by an attack that causes you to become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to split into two identical Oozes. Each Ooze uses your game statistics, except that they are one size category smaller than your normal size, and your remaining {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} Maximum are divided evenly between the two Oozes (round down). Your equipment is not duplicated; all of it remains on one of the Oozes.
Legion of Slime.entries.2: Each Ooze acts on your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}. They can each move independently, but they can only collectively take one action and {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn and one {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} per round.
Legion of Slime.entries.3: After you use this feature, you cannot use it again until after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Legion of Slime.name: Legion of Slime
Lessons of the First Ones.entries.0: You have received knowledge from an elder entity of the multiverse, allowing you to gain one {@filter Origin feat|feats|category=o} of your choice.
Lessons of the First Ones.entries.1.entr: You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different Origin feat.
Lessons of the First Ones.name: Lessons of the First Ones
Lich Magica.entries.0: You can trade the soul in your soul vessel for increased magical power.
Lich Magica.entries.1: On your turn, as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend the charge in your soul vessel to gain one of the following effects:
Lich Magica.entries.2.items.0: Regain a spell slot equal to or lower than your current Lich Transformation Stage.
Lich Magica.entries.2.items.1: The next saving throw made before the end of your next turn against one of your spells has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.
Lich Magica.entries.2.items.2: The next time you cast a spell before the end of your next turn, deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Necrotic damage per Lich Transformation Stage to one creature damaged by that spell.
Lich Magica.entries.2.items.3: The next time you cast a spell before the end of your next turn that restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, that spell restores an additional {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} per Lich Transformation Stage.
Lich Magica.name: Lich Magica
Lifedrinker.entries.0: Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage (your choice) to the creature, and you can expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} to roll it and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll plus your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}).
Lifedrinker.name: Lifedrinker
Light Armor Training.entries.0: Dealing with the threats you face requires the right combination of protection and movement. You have training with Light armor.
Light Armor Training.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, your AC increases by 1 while wearing Light armor.
Light Armor Training.entries.1.name: Light Armor Expertise
Light Armor Training.name: Light Armor Training
Liquid Courage.entries.0: You gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Fighter level plus your Constitution modifier. If any of these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} remain after 1 minute, they vanish.
Liquid Courage.name: Liquid Courage
Liquid Rage.entries.0: For 1 minute, your attacks with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal an extra {@damage 1d4} damage on hit.
Liquid Rage.name: Liquid Rage
Lord of Undeath.entries.0: When you use {@optfeature Acolyte of Undeath|GrimHollowPG24} you can choose to transform the target into a {@creature Ghoul|XMM} instead of a {@creature Zombie|XMM}.
Lord of Undeath.name: Lord of Undeath
Lucky.entries.0: The luck you carry will see you through the worst Etharis has to offer. When you roll a 1 on a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can reroll that die but must use the new roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Lucky.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the reroll made with Lucky.
Lucky.entries.1.name: Master of Fortune
Lucky.name: Lucky
Lunging Attack.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one Superiority Die and take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action. If you move at least 5 feet in a straight line immediately before hitting with a melee attack as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on this turn, you can add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll.
Lunging Attack.name: Lunging Attack
Lust for the Hunt.entries.0: The savage nature of your curse sometimes causes you to lose control. These ferocious tendencies are a constant struggle between you and the beast within.
Lust for the Hunt.entries.1: The first time you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must succeed on a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. If you are in the light of a full moon, you automatically fail this saving throw.
Lust for the Hunt.entries.2: If you fail this saving throw, you are subjected to the following rules until you can end the hybrid form:
Lust for the Hunt.entries.3.items.0: You immediately enter your hybrid form. You go completely feral, and you cannot use any of your equipment or class features.
Lust for the Hunt.entries.3.items.1: On your turn while in hybrid form, you must move toward the closest creature you can see, smell, or hear, prioritizing {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} creatures. If you end your movement and no creature is within 5 feet of you, you must use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action to move toward the closest one.
Lust for the Hunt.entries.3.items.2: If there is a creature within 5 feet of you and you have not used your action, you must make an {@action Attack|XPHB} action using your Claw attack.
Lust for the Hunt.entries.3.items.3: When you damage a creature on your turn and it is not reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you must use further attacks that turn against that creature.
Lust for the Hunt.entries.4: If you are at full {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of your turn, you can make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a success, you return to your normal form. You also return to your normal form if {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} is cast on you, or after 1 hour passes.
Lust for the Hunt.name: Lust for the Hunt
Maddening Hex.entries.0: As a bonus action, you cause a psychic disturbance around the target cursed by your {@spell hex} spell or by a warlock feature of yours, such as Hexblade's Curse or Sign of Ill Omen. When you do so, you deal psychic damage to the cursed target and each creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of it. The psychic damage equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 damage). To use this invocation, you must be able to see the cursed target, and it must be within 30 feet of you.
Maddening Hex.name: Maddening Hex
Magic Resistance.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells.
Magic Resistance.name: Magic Resistance
Magic Tricks.entries.0: Your Fey masters provide you with the ability to weave magic based on your Fey Court allegiance. You gain the ability to cast one cantrip, one level 1 spell, and one level 2 spell. You can cast each of these once without using a spell slot or needing spell components. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The DC for these spells is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Magic Tricks.entries.1.entries.0: Your cantrip is {@spell Poison Spray|XPHB}, and your spells are {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} and {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}.
Magic Tricks.entries.1.name: Spring
Magic Tricks.entries.2.entries.0: Your cantrip is {@spell Fire Bolt|XPHB}, and your spells are {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} and {@spell Flame Blade|XPHB}.
Magic Tricks.entries.2.name: Summer
Magic Tricks.entries.3.entries.0: Your cantrip is {@spell Shillelagh|XPHB}, and your spells are {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} and {@spell Ray of Enfeeblement|XPHB}.
Magic Tricks.entries.3.name: Autumn
Magic Tricks.entries.4.entries.0: Your cantrip is {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB}, and your spells are {@spell Ice Knife|XPHB} and {@spell Darkness|XPHB}.
Magic Tricks.entries.4.name: Winter
Magic Tricks.name: Magic Tricks
Magical Fortification.entries.0: The more that magic threatens you, the more your resilience to it increases. Choose an ability score: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws using that ability score against spells and other magical effects.
If you take this trait multiple times, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws using a new ability score each time.

Additionally, if you fail a saving throw against a spell or other magical effect and you do not have proficiency with that saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reroll the save. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Magical Fortification.entries.1.name: Extended Fortification
Magical Fortification.name: Magical Fortification
Magical Insight.entries.0: Magic is power in the right hands, and those hands are yours. You have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} skill.
Magical Insight.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Arcana|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Magical Insight.entries.1.name: Magical Historian
Magical Insight.name: Magical Insight
Magical Savvy.entries.0: Whether through intensive study or the innate touch of magic in your blood, you have the ability to invoke magical spells. You learn one cantrip of your choice from any spell list, which you cast using the associated ability score: Intelligence for Wizard spells, Wisdom for Cleric and Druid spells, and Charisma for Bard, Sorcerer, and Warlock spells. If the spell appears on multiple spell lists, choose one to determine the spellcasting attribute for that spell.
Magical Savvy.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait multiple times, you select a different cantrip each time, or you can select a level 1 spell from the same list as a cantrip you have previously chosen. If you select a level 1 spell, you can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If you have levels in the associated spellcasting class, you always have this spell prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.
Magical Savvy.entries.1.name: Magical Savant
Magical Savvy.name: Magical Savvy
Maneuvering Attack.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to maneuver one of your comrades into another position. Add the Superiority Die roll to the attack's damage roll, and choose a willing creature who can see or hear you. That creature can use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} from the target of your attack.
Maneuvering Attack.name: Maneuvering Attack
Manipulate Objects.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action.
Manipulate Objects.name: Manipulate Objects
Mask of Many Faces.entries.0: You can cast {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} without expending a spell slot.
Mask of Many Faces.name: Mask of Many Faces
Master of Distraction.entries.0: You draw your foes' attention to you, intending it to be the last diversion they ever see. As an {@action Influence|XPHB} action, you put on a tactical display (bravado, cowardice, confusion, or some other tactic) that gets your enemies' attention. Until the end of your next turn, any attack on an enemy within 10 feet of you that could see you when you took the {@action Influence|XPHB} action, is made with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
If you take this trait twice, when you use Master of Distraction, one affected enemy of your choice also has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls it makes against any of your allies until the end of your next turn.
Master of Distraction.entries.1.name: Hindering Distraction
Master of Distraction.name: Master of Distraction
Master of Many.entries.0: You add a third elemental to your {@optfeature Elemental Affinity|GrimHollowPG24|Elemental Affinity Boon}. When you do so, you gain the associated benefits of your new element.
Master of Many.name: Master of Many
Master of Myriad Forms.entries.0: You can cast {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} without expending a spell slot.
Master of Myriad Forms.name: Master of Myriad Forms
Master of Undeath.entries.0: Undead creatures under your control from a Lich Transformation Boon have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls when they are within 5 feet of you.
Master of Undeath.entries.1: Additionally, whenever an Undead creature you control due to a Lich Transformation Boon is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can spend the charge of your soul vessel as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. If you do, that Undead creature is at 1 hit point instead and may immediately move up to its full speed and make an {@action Attack|XPHB} action with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. This feature has no effect if the creature has been reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by Radiant damage.
Master of Undeath.name: Master of Undeath
Master of the Green Sisterhood.entries.0: You come into your power as a Hag and enhance your abilities.
Master of the Green Sisterhood.entries.1: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you may make an additional Claw attack. Your Claw attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d6} Poison damage.

You learn a corrupt ritual to create a Hag Eye or a {@creature Gasdra|GrimHollowPG24} companion. The ritual takes four hours and 200 GP to complete. You choose which ritual you know when you select this boon. You can only have one Hag Eye or Gasdra companion at a time, and you cannot have both.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can recover an expended spell slot by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} equal to the spell slot's level. The spell slot can have a level equal to no more than one third your character level (round up). Once you use this boon, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Master of the Green Sisterhood.name: Master of the Green Sisterhood
Master of the Red Sisterhood.entries.0: You come into your power as a Hag and enhance your abilities.
Master of the Red Sisterhood.entries.1.i: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you may make an additional Claw attack. Your Claw attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage. You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Psychic damage dealt.

Your banter becomes supernaturally charming. A creature under your magical influence (such as being {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you) who is able to hear you for 1 minute must must make a Charisma saving throw with a DC equal to 14 plus your Transformation Stage. On a failed save, when your mental influence ends, the target forgets magic was used to influence it. You regain use of this feature after you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} to cast the {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} spell.
Master of the Red Sisterhood.name: Master of the Red Sisterhood
Master of the Sea Sisterhood.entries.0: You come into your power as a Hag and enhance your abilities.
Master of the Sea Sisterhood.entries.1.i: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you may make an additional Claw attack. Your Claw attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d6} Slashing damage.

Your appearance becomes so vile that it terrifies mortal creatures. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gaze upon a Beast or Humanoid within 30 feet that can see your true form. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 14 plus your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn. A target that succeeds is immune to this feature for 24 hours. You regain use of this feature after you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend and roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} to touch a creature and allow the target to breathe water for a number of minutes equal to the amount rolled.
Master of the Sea Sisterhood.name: Master of the Sea Sisterhood
Masterful Aptitude.entries.0: Your discipline and focus give you an edge that others lack. Choose one of your skill or tool proficiencies. You have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} on ability checks made using the chosen proficiency.
Masterful Aptitude.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain its benefit for a new skill proficiency or tool proficiency each time.
Masterful Aptitude.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, when you make a check using a skill or tool for which you've taken Masterful Aptitude, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Masterful Aptitude.entries.1.name: Focused Mastery
Masterful Aptitude.name: Masterful Aptitude
Maverick Spirit.entries.0: When you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma ability check or saving throw, you can expend one {@classFeature Risk|Gunslinger|ValdaGunslinger|2|ValdaGunslinger|Risk Die} to add it to the roll, potentially turning it into a success. You can only use this maneuver once per turn.
Maverick Spirit.name: Maverick Spirit
Meditative Rest.entries.0: Sleep is a luxury you've never needed to afford. When you rest, you meditate deeply for 4 hours, dreaming but remaining conscious. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that other humanoids do from 8 hours of sleep.
Meditative Rest.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you need to spend only 2 hours in your meditation to gain the benefit of 8 hours of sleep, and you gain a {@dice d6} at the end of each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Before the end of your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can roll the {@dice d6} and add it to any {@dice d20} Test you make. You can decide to roll the {@dice d6} after the {@dice d20} Test is made, but you must do so before the outcome of the roll is known.
Meditative Rest.entries.1.name: Restorative Rest
Meditative Rest.name: Meditative Rest
Medium Armor Training.entries.0: The pounding you routinely take in combat requires a formidable layer of defense. You have training with Medium armor and with Shields.
If you take this trait twice, you have training with Heavy armor.
Medium Armor Training.entries.1.name: Heavy Armor Training
Medium Armor Training.name: Medium Armor Training
Melted Appearance.entries.0: Your body is now a hideous lump of dripping flesh excreting foul smelling slime. While your form remains roughly humanoid, the horror of what you are is clear to anyone that looks upon your molten face. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} when you use the {@action Influence|XPHB} action to make Charisma-based ability checks made against creatures that are not Aberrations or Oozes.
Melted Appearance.entries.1: In addition, your Transformation to an Ooze has liquefied many of your organs and dimmed your senses. Your eyes melt and run down your face and there is nothing but flesh where your ears once were. You permanently have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition beyond the range of your {@sense Blindsight|XPHB}.
Melted Appearance.name: Melted Appearance
Memori Lichdom.entries.0: While your soul vessel is charged, you gain the following benefits to your weapon attacks or {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB}:
Memori Lichdom.entries.1.items.1: They have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} if taken as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
Memori Lichdom.entries.1.items.2: After you deal damage with a weapon attack or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can spend a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later on that turn and expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}. Roll the die and deal Necrotic damage equal to the result to one target damaged by the triggering attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Memori Lichdom.name: Memori Lichdom
Menacing Attack.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to frighten the target. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.
Menacing Attack.name: Menacing Attack
Menacing Roar.entries.0: Your battle cry can cause even the most formidable foes to quail before you. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you emit a roar, shout, or other loud vocal outburst. Each creature of your choice within 10 feet of you that can hear you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your Constitution modifier) or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condtion until the end of your next turn. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Menacing Roar.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Menacing Roar, one target of your choice has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.
Menacing Roar.entries.1.name: Incomparable Roar
Menacing Roar.name: Menacing Roar
For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and saving throws. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls using Strength, and you deal {@damage 1d6} extra damage to targets you hit with a Strength-based attack. The damage is the same type dealt by the attack.
Might (Beast, Construct, Giant).name: Might (Beast, Construct, Giant)
Mighty Shove.entries.0: Your powerful blows send your targets reeling. When you hit a creature no more than one size larger than you with a melee attack, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to attempt to shove that creature. The target must succeed on a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or be pushed up to 10 feet away from you.
Mighty Shove.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Mighty Shove, the target creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.
Mighty Shove.entries.1.name: Overwhelming Shove
Mighty Shove.name: Mighty Shove
Mimic Object.entries.0: When you manifest your Ooze Form, it lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Mimic Object.entries.1: While manifesting your Ooze Form, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to transform into an object of your size or up to two sizes smaller. Any equipment worn or carried is dropped in your space. While mimicking an object, you retain your statistics.
Mimic Object.entries.2: While mimicking an object, you are indistinguishable from the object and you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you are touched to adhere to the creature that touches you. The creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition. The DC to escape is equal to DC equal to 8 plus your Constitution modifier plus your Transformation Stage. Ability checks made to escape this grapple have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.
Mimic Object.name: Mimic Object
Mindful Investigator.entries.0: Putting together the pieces of even the darkest mysteries is second nature to you. You have proficiency in the {@skill Investigation|XPHB} skill.
Mindful Investigator.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Investigation|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Mindful Investigator.entries.1.name: Thorough Sleuth
Mindful Investigator.name: Mindful Investigator
Minions of Chaos.entries.0: You can cast {@spell conjure elemental} once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Minions of Chaos.name: Minions of Chaos
Mire the Mind.entries.0: You can cast {@spell slow} once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Mire the Mind.name: Mire the Mind
Mist Form.entries.0: You can cast the {@spell Gaseous Form|XPHB} spell a number of times equal to your Vampire Transformation Stage without using a spell slot or needing to use Verbal, Somatic, or Material components. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Mist Form.entries.1: You can cast this spell as an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} on your turn, or as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you would take Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage. You cast the spell before taking the damage.
Mist Form.name: Mist Form
Mist Stance.entries.0: You can spend 4 ki points to cast {@spell gaseous form}, targeting yourself.
Mist Stance.name: Mist Stance
Misty Visions.entries.0: You can cast {@spell Silent Image|XPHB} without expending a spell slot.
Misty Visions.name: Misty Visions
Monk.entries.0: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}, and you can roll {@dice 1d6} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. In addition, after you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action or take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make one attack with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Monk.name: Монах
Moved by Faith.entries.0: The grimmest myths and legends of the past hold the keys to shaping the future. You have proficiency in the {@skill Religion|XPHB} skill.
Moved by Faith.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Religion|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Moved by Faith.entries.1.name: Force of Faith
Moved by Faith.name: Moved by Faith
Multiattack.entries.1: You can attack twice with a Wild Shape form's attack instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.
Multiattack.name: Multiattack
Mutable Corpus.entries.0: You have abundant control over your Ooze Form. Each time you manifest your Ooze Form, choose one of the following augmentations:
Mutable Corpus.entries.1.items.0: {@i Breathless}. You do not need to breathe.
Mutable Corpus.entries.1.items.1: {@i Disguise}. You remold your features to look different. You can change your apparent species, seem 1 foot shorter or taller, and appear heavier or lighter (you must keep the same basic arrangement of limbs). To discern the disguise, a creature must take the {@action Study|XPHB} action and succeed on an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check against DC 10 + your Constitution modifier + your Transformation Stage.
Mutable Corpus.entries.1.items.2: {@i Transparent}. Your body becomes transparent. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.
Mutable Corpus.name: Mutable Corpus
Mystic Monster.entries.1: You can cast spells while you're in this Wild Shape form. If you have 18 or more Druid levels, creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells you cast while this Mutation is active.
Mystic Monster.name: Mystic Monster
Natural Armor.entries.1: You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.
Natural Armor.entries.2.entries.0: You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times.
Natural Armor.entries.2.name: Повторяемое
Natural Armor.name: Natural Armor
Natural Attack.entries.0: The gift of natural weaponry means you are never unarmed, as your foes learn to their peril. Your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Strength or Dexterity modifier. The type of damage dealt by your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} can be Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing, based on the type of natural weaponry you possess (claws, horns, a tail, and so forth).
Natural Attack.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can use {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Natural Attack.entries.1.name: Swift Strike
Natural Attack.name: Natural Attack
Natural Camouflage.entries.0: Your ability to fade into the background of familiar territory helps keep you safe from threats. Choose an environment:  arctic, coastal, desert, forest, grassland, hill and mountain, swamp, subterranean, or underwater. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Stealth checks made with the {@action Hide|XPHB} action while in that environment.
Natural Camouflage.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain its benefits for a new environment each time.
Natural Camouflage.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, when you take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action, you can forgo making a {@skill Stealth|XPHB} check while in any environment chosen with Natural Camouflage, instead treating the check as if you had rolled a 15. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Natural Camouflage.entries.1.name: Shared Camouflage
Natural Camouflage.name: Natural Camouflage
Natural Movement.entries.0: The time you've spent in the natural world lets you travel at speed, and hinders the abilities of those who would hunt you. Choose an environment:  arctic, coastal, desert, forest, grassland, hill and mountain, swamp, subterranean, or underwater. While in that environment, moving through nonmagical {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} costs you no extra movement, and ability checks made to track you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.
Natural Movement.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain its benefits for a new environment each time. Additionally, while in any environment chosen for Natural Movement, as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can grant creatures of your choice the benefit of Natural Movement for 1 hour, as long as those creatures remain within 120 feet of you and can see you.
Natural Movement.entries.1.name: Shared Movement
Natural Movement.name: Natural Movement
Natural Weapons (Beast, Dragon, Monstros: For 1 hour, you gain natural weapons. You can roll {@dice 1d6} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. This damage can be Piercing or Slashing. You choose each time you make an attack. You can also make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your Unarmed Strikes.
Nature's Voice.entries.0: Mastering the subtle expression of fauna and flora grants you an edge in dealing with the threats of the wilderness. Through sounds and gestures, you can communicate simple ideas with Beasts and Plant creatures, understanding if a creature is hungry, for example. This gives you no specific ability to control such creatures, and you can't understand or learn detailed information from them.
Nature's Voice.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made as part of an {@action Influence|XPHB} action to interact with a Beast or Plant creature.
Nature's Voice.entries.1.name: Primal Voice
Nature's Voice.name: Nature's Voice
Necromantic Dystrophia.entries.0: When your soul vessel is not charged, you suffer the following effects:
Necromantic Dystrophia.entries.1.items.0: You cannot use the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Dodge|XPHB}, or {@action Disengage|XPHB} actions.
Necromantic Dystrophia.entries.1.items.2: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws.
Necromantic Dystrophia.entries.1.items.3: You cannot disguise your {@optfeature Hideous Appearance (Lich)|GrimHollowPG24|Hideous Appearance}.
Necromantic Dystrophia.entries.2: Upon charging your soul vessel with an appropriate soul, you are no longer subject to these effects.
Necromantic Dystrophia.name: Necromantic Dystrophia
Nimble Moves.entries.0: You have learned the value of being able to manipulate the world around you without attracting the notice of others. You have proficiency in the {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB} skill.
Nimble Moves.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Nimble Moves.entries.1.name: Exquisite Legerdemain
Nimble Moves.name: Nimble Moves
One with Shadows.entries.0: While you're in an area of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can cast {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} on yourself without expending a spell slot.
One with Shadows.name: One with Shadows
Ooze Form.entries.0: You become an Ooze in addition to any other creature type(s) you are. You gain {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet. If you already have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB}, your range increases by 30 feet.
Ooze Form.entries.1: You can will your body to melt and flow, becoming more fluid than solid. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you manifest your Ooze Form. You become amorphous, able to move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement, and you are {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions. You remain in Ooze Form for 1 minute or until you use your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to return to normal.
Ooze Form.entries.2: You can manifest your Ooze Form a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation stage. You regain all uses of this feature after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Ooze Form.name: Ooze Form
Otherworldly Leap.entries.0: You can cast {@spell Jump|XPHB} on yourself without expending a spell slot.
Otherworldly Leap.name: Otherworldly Leap
Out of Phase.entries.0: Your corporeal presence shifts and fades, softening your enemies' ability to harm you. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, for 1 minute, all creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you, and you can move through other creature's spaces without treating them as {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Out of Phase.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Out of Phase, you can extend its benefit to any ally within 10 feet of you.
Out of Phase.entries.1.name: Phase Shift
Out of Phase.name: Out of Phase
Overwhelming Brand.entries.0: You can apply one of the following additional effects to a creature under the effect of your {@optfeature Daemonic Brand|GrimHollowPG24}.
Overwhelming Brand.entries.1.items.0: The creature's {@variantrule Speed|GrimHollowPG24} is halved, and you can move the creature up to 10 feet horizontally on your turn (no action required).
Overwhelming Brand.entries.1.items.1: The creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition. On your turn, you can choose to remove this condition (no action required).
Overwhelming Brand.name: Overwhelming Brand
Pack Hunter.entries.0: Fighting in the thick of battle lets you aid your allies when it counts. When an ally within 10 feet of you is about to make an attack roll or a saving throw, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to grant that ally {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack or save. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Pack Hunter.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, Pack Hunter can be triggered by any ally within 30 feet of you. Additionally, if the attack roll misses or the saving throw fails, you don't lose that usage of Pack Hunter.
Pack Hunter.entries.1.name: Pack Leader
Pack Hunter.name: Pack Hunter
Pack Tactics.entries.0: Staying close to your allies in combat makes you even more dangerous. When you start your turn with at least one ally who isn't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} within 5 feet of another creature you can see, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature until the end of your turn.
Pack Tactics.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, gaining {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} from Pack Tactics requires no action.
Pack Tactics.entries.1.name: Pack Instinct
Pack Tactics.name: Pack Tactics
Pact of the Blade.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can conjure a pact weapon in your hand—a Simple or Martial Melee weapon of your choice with which you bond—or create a bond with a magic weapon you touch; you can't bond with a magic weapon if someone else is attuned to it or another Warlock is bonded with it. Until the bond ends, you have proficiency with the weapon, and you can use it as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}.
Pact of the Blade.entries.1: Whenever you attack with the bonded weapon, you can use your Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity; and you can cause the weapon to deal Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage or its normal damage type.
Pact of the Blade.entries.2: Your bond with the weapon ends if you use this feature's {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} again, if the weapon is more than 5 feet away from you for 1 minute or more, or if you die. A conjured weapon disappears when the bond ends.
Pact of the Blade.name: Pact of the Blade
Pact of the Chain.entries.0: You learn the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell and can cast it as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action without expending a spell slot.
Pact of the Chain.entries.1: When you cast the spell, you choose one of the normal forms for your familiar or one of the following special forms: {@creature Imp|XMM}, {@creature Pseudodragon|XMM}, {@creature Quasit|XMM}, {@creature Skeleton|XMM}, {@creature Slaad Tadpole|XMM}, {@creature Sphinx of Wonder|XMM}, {@creature Sprite|XMM}, or {@creature Venomous Snake|XMM} (see appendix B for the familiar's stat block).
Pact of the Chain.entries.2: Additionally, when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can forgo one of your own attacks to allow your familiar to make one attack of its own with its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
Pact of the Chain.name: Pact of the Chain
Pact of the Talisman.entries.0: Your patron gives you an amulet, a talisman that can aid the wearer when the need is great. When the wearer fails an ability check, they can add a {@dice d4} to the roll, potentially turning the roll into a success. This benefit can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and all expended uses are restored when you finish a long rest.
Pact of the Talisman.entries.1: If you lose the talisman, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony can be performed during a short or long rest, and it destroys the previous amulet. The talisman turns to ash when you die.
Pact of the Talisman.name: Pact of the Talisman
Pact of the Tome.entries.0: Stitching together strands of shadow, you conjure forth a book in your hand at the end of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. This Book of Shadows (you determine its appearance) contains eldritch magic that only you can access, granting you the benefits below. The book disappears if you conjure another book with this feature or if you die.
Pact of the Tome.entries.1.entries.0.ent: When the book appears, choose three {@filter cantrips|spells|level=0}, and choose two {@filter level 1 spells|spells|level=1|components & miscellaneous=ritual} that have the {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB} tag. The spells can be from any class's spell list, and they must be spells you don't already have prepared. While the book is on your person, you have the chosen spells prepared, and they function as Warlock spells for you.
Pact of the Tome.entries.1.entries.1.ent: You can use the book as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}.
Pact of the Tome.name: Pact of the Tome
Paladin.entries.0: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend a spell slot to deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage to the target, plus another {@dice 1d6} per level of the spell slot.
Paladin.entries.1: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} if the target is a Fiend or an Undead.
Paladin.entries.2: Once you use this talent, you must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use it again.
Paladin.name: Паладин
Paralyzing Touch.entries.0: Your Ghastly Touch feature takes on a spirit-freezing quality. The damage from your {@optfeature Ghastly Touch|GrimHollowPG24} increases to {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage. Additionally, you can force any creature you damage with {@optfeature Ghastly Touch|GrimHollowPG24} to make a Constitution saving throw. The DC for this saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Paralyzing Touch.entries.1: On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, they have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Paralyzing Touch.name: Paralyzing Touch
Parry.entries.0: When another creature damages you with a melee attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend one Superiority Die to reduce the damage by the number you roll on your Superiority Die plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).
Parry.name: Parry
Pass Through.entries.0: Making use of constant movement lets you minimize the threat of larger foes. You can move through the space of any creature at least on size larger than you.
Pass Through.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you do not treat another creature's space as {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
Pass Through.entries.1.name: Nimble Passage
Pass Through.name: Pass Through
Personal Bastion.entries.0: Focusing all your resolve, you stand fast and watch your enemies flail against your defenses. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you become motionless and gain the following effects:
Personal Bastion.entries.1.items.0: You have a +4 bonus to AC.
Personal Bastion.entries.1.items.1: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.
Personal Bastion.entries.1.items.2: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength and Constitution saving throws.
Personal Bastion.entries.1.items.3: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0 and you can't benefit from any bonus to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Personal Bastion.entries.2: You can't take actions, and you can't use your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} except to end the effect of this trait.
Personal Bastion.entries.3.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Personal Bastion, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to half your normal {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} (rounded down), you do not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws, and you can use {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Bonus Actions}.
Personal Bastion.entries.3.name: Mobile Bastion
Personal Bastion.name: Personal Bastion
Persuasive Knack.entries.0: You have learned that the best way to deal with certain threats is to keep those threats from escalating. You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} skill.
Persuasive Knack.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Persuasive Knack.entries.1.name: Tongue of Gold
Persuasive Knack.name: Persuasive Knack
You have powdered your armor with fey dust or sewn a pelt of a phase-shifting monstrosity into it. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.
Phase Leap (Armor Modification).name: Phase Leap (Armor Modification)
Physical Deterioration.entries.0: Your skin sloughs away in thick, syrupy sheets and your bones become like melted wax. The sun itself burns you, and holy light becomes lethal. Now your reactions slow as much as your pace.
Physical Deterioration.entries.1: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Radiant damage, and you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ability checks and saving throws.
Physical Deterioration.name: Physical Deterioration
Piercing Arrow.entries.0: You use transmutation magic to give your arrow an ethereal quality. When you use this option, you don't make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, the arrow shoots forward in a line, which is 1 foot wide and 30 feet long, before disappearing. The arrow passes harmlessly through objects, ignoring cover. Each creature in that line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage as if it were hit by the arrow, plus an extra {@damage 1d6} piercing damage. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage.
Piercing Arrow.entries.1: The piercing damage increases to {@damage 2d6} when you reach 18th level in this class.
Piercing Arrow.name: Piercing Arrow
Planar Binding (Fey).entries.0: Your body and soul have been saturated with the magical energy of the Realm of Faerie. Rather than inuring you to the essence of magic, it has made you more susceptible.
Planar Binding (Fey).entries.1: After finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first saving throw you make against a spell originating from an enemy.
Planar Binding (Fey).name: Planar Binding (Fey)
Planar Binding (Primordial).entries.0: Your body and soul are connected to the Elemental Plane. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the plane attempts to pull you into it.
Planar Binding (Primordial).name: Planar Binding (Primordial)
Planar Binding (Seraph).entries.0: Your body and soul are bound to the higher planes of good. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the plane attempts to pull your soul into its place of final reward.
Planar Binding (Seraph).name: Planar Binding (Seraph)
Poison Resilience.entries.0: Your exceptional fortitude lets you shrug off the effects of even the worst toxins. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.
Poison Resilience.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against being {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Poison Resilience.entries.1.name: Poison Indemnity
Poison Resilience.name: Poison Resilience
Poison Tendrils.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage and are {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
Poison Tendrils.entries.1: While your Writhing Tendrils are active, any creature you choose that starts its turn within 5 feet of you takes {@damage 3d6} Poison damage unless they succeed on a Constitution saving throw. The DC for this saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Poison Tendrils.name: Poison Tendrils
Polyglot.entries.0: The advantages of mastering the languages of enemies and allies alike are clear to you. You learn two languages of your choice.
Polyglot.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait multiple times, you learn two new languages each time.
Polyglot.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@action Influence|XPHB} action ability checks made to interact with another creature using any language you selected with Polyglot. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Polyglot.entries.1.name: Language Expert
Polyglot.name: Polyglot
Possession.entries.0: {@action Magic|XPHB|Магическим} действием вы входите в пространство Гуманоида или Зверя и заставляете это существо совершить спасбросок Харизмы со Сл 15. При провале вы исчезаете, а цель получает состояние {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB|Недееспособен} и теряет контроль над своим телом. Вы контролируете тело, не лишая цель сознания.
Possession.entries.1: Пока вы одержимаете цель, вы не можете быть целью атак, заклинаний или других эффектов. Вы сохраняете своё мировоззрение, Интеллект, Мудрость, Харизму и любой {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Иммунитет} к состояниям {@condition Charmed|XPHB|Очарован} и {@condition Frightened|XPHB|Испуган}. В остальном вы используете характеристики одержимой цели, но не получаете доступ к её знаниям, способностям к сотворению заклинаний или {@action Magic|XPHB|Магическим} действиям, классовым умениям или владениям.
Possession.entries.2: Одержимость длится 1 час и заканчивается, если {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Хиты} одержимой цели опускаются до 0, если вы прекращаете её {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием} или если вас изгоняет эффект, снимающий одержимость. Когда одержимость заканчивается, вы появляетесь в свободном пространстве в пределах 5 футов от одержимого существа. Использовав эту способность, вы не можете применить её снова, пока не завершите {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Короткий} или {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Длинный отдых}.
Possession.name: Одержимость
Power Nap.entries.0: When you don't know how long it might be before your next full respite, you learn to take maximum advantage of any rest you can get. When taking a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can choose to sleep for 1 hour. If you do so, you reduce your {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} by one level and regain a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} in addition to the other benefits of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.
Power Nap.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when using Power Nap, you can choose to regain a single resource that would normally refresh on a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. For example, a Sorcerer could choose to regain a Sorcery Point on a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.
Power Nap.entries.1.name: Extreme Resilience
Power Nap.name: Power Nap
Powerful Build.entries.0: Whether carrying well-earned loot or the body of a fallen companion, you shoulder that load with ease. You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift. A Small creature with this trait can use any weapon with {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} as long as they have proficiency with that weapon. (This is an Exploration trait.)
Powerful Build.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can move or knock foes {@condition prone} with ease. When you use {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} to shove a creature 5 feet or give it the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw. (This is a Combat trait.)
Powerful Build.entries.1.name: Powerful Shove
Powerful Build.name: Powerful Build
Precision Attack.entries.0: When you miss with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die, roll that die, and add it to the attack roll, potentially causing the attack to hit.
Precision Attack.name: Precision Attack
Preeminent in House.entries.0: When you request a favor from your house (as decribed under "{@optfeature Favored in House|EFA}"), it can take one of the following additional forms:
Preeminent in House.entries.1.items.0: A dragonmarked member of the house casts for you a level 5 spell associated with the house's mark at no cost.
Preeminent in House.entries.1.items.1: A member of the house casts for you a different spell of level 8 or lower at half the usual cost.
Preeminent in House.entries.2: In addition, your house stipend increases to support your Aristocratic lifestyle (10 GP per day).
Preeminent in House.name: Preeminent in House
Presto Powder.entries.0: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls and you can take the {@action Dash|XPHB} or {@action Disengage|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Presto Powder.name: Presto Powder
Primeval Body.entries.0: Your transformation to an Elemental creature has changed your body entirely. You no longer need to sleep, breathe, or eat. Also, you no longer age normally and suffer no effects of aging.
Primeval Body.entries.1: Additionally, choose one damage type from Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, and Lightning damage. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type. If you already have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to it.
Primeval Body.name: Primeval Body
Primordial Aura.entries.0: Creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, or Lightning damage. You choose the damage type after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, and you can change the damage type as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. If they already have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to any of these damage types, they gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage type.
Primordial Aura.name: Primordial Aura
Primordial Chaos.entries.0: The plane of Primordial energy that you're tied to strengthens its pull on you. Whenever you roll a natural 1 on a saving throw against a spell, you take {@damage 8d6} Force damage, which ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities}. This damage is in addition to any normal damage from the effect.
Primordial Chaos.name: Primordial Chaos
Primordial Form.entries.0: You become an Elemental in addition to any other creature type(s).
Primordial Form.entries.1: Your Constitution score increases by 1, but cannot increase above 20 in this way.
Primordial Form.name: Primordial Form
Protection of the Talisman.entries.0: When the wearer of your talisman fails a saving throw, they can add a {@dice d4} to the roll, potentially turning the save into a success. This benefit can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and all expended uses are restored when you finish a long rest.
Protection of the Talisman.name: Protection of the Talisman
Protective Wings.entries.0: While you are manifesting your Angelic Wings, you gain the following benefits:
Protective Wings.entries.1.items.0: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, when you or an ally within 5 feet of you is targeted by a weapon attack, you can impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that attack. If the attack hits the ally, you take half the attack's damage (rounded up) and the original target takes the other half.
Protective Wings.entries.1.items.1: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws or ability checks to avoid having the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition or to escape a Grapple.
Protective Wings.entries.1.items.2: Your {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} is increased by 10 feet.
Protective Wings.name: Protective Wings
Psychic Spirit.entries.0: Your strength of mind shields you from unnatural forces. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage.
Psychic Spirit.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against an effect that deals Psychic damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Psychic Spirit.entries.1.name: Spirit's Strength
Psychic Spirit.name: Psychic Spirit
Pull of Oblivion.entries.0: Your connection to the material realm becomes gossamer thin. When you roll a natural 1 on a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you take {@damage 4d6} Force damage, and this damage cannot be mitigated. If this damage reduces you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you die.
Pull of Oblivion.entries.1: When you die in this way or any other, you can only be brought back to life through magic equivalent to level 7 or higher spells.
Pull of Oblivion.name: Pull of Oblivion
Pull of the Netherworld.entries.0: Your new native plane attempts to pull you to it, laying claim to your form. Whenever you roll a natural 1 on a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you take {@damage 6d6} Psychic damage as the denizens of the fiendish plane attempt to unbind your soul from the Material Plane. This damage can only be taken once per {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short}, and it ignores all {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities}. If this damage reduces your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to 0, you die and your soul is immediately taken to the Netherworld. You can only be returned to life by spells of 7th level or higher.
Pull of the Netherworld.name: Pull of the Netherworld
Purity's Pain.entries.0: Unspoiled, pure things cause you physical pain to witness. A child hugging their parent, a loyal dog laying its head upon a sick man's lap, a work of religious art devoted to an Arch Seraph, a baby's laughter: these things make you physically ill.
Purity's Pain.entries.1: When you are in the presence of a pure act or object (GM's discretion) you are wracked with pain. At the start of each of your turns while you can see the cause of your pain, you suffer {@dice 3d6} plus your Charisma modifier Psychic damage, and cannot use a Hag Transformation boons until the start of your next turn. The first time you take Psychic damage from this flaw after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. If you fail, you have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition as long as you can see the source of your pain.
Purity's Pain.name: Purity's Pain
Pushing Attack.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to drive the target back. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet directly away from you.
Pushing Attack.name: Pushing Attack
Queen's Command.entries.0: The Fey court lends you its power, and in exchange you must pay regular tribute to maintain your freedom. Twice a year, at times relevant to the Fey Court that you serve—such as the solstices or the equinoxes—you must deliver a treasure or other significant tribute to your ruling Fey Queen.
Queen's Command.entries.1: If your queen considers the tribute lacking, you are immediately notified of your failure and are given detailed instructions on how to atone. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} until you rectify the situation. If you do not satisfy your queen's demand in one week's time, your queen transports you immediately to her Fey Court, where you must answer for your insolence. Once transported in this way, you must remain in the Realm of Faerie for 100 years, or until you strike a bargain with your queen to let you return to the Material Plane.
Queen's Command.entries.2: Your GM will tell you what constitutes an appropriate tribute to the court. Some Fey Queens prefer magic items while others wish for long-kept secrets, the capture of Fey criminals, or Humanoid servants.
Queen's Command.name: Queen's Command
Quick Initiative.entries.0: Danger is never far away from you, and you are always ready for it. You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.
Quick Initiative.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower as if you rolled a 10.
Quick Initiative.entries.1.name: Focused Initiative
Quick Initiative.name: Quick Initiative
Quick Slip.entries.0: Even in the thick of battle, anything that obscures your enemies' view of you gives you a chance to strike unseen. You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on each of your turns. You must have appropriate cover to attempt to hide, as normal.
Quick Slip.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the Stealth checks you make with the {@action Hide|XPHB} action when you use Quick Slip.
Quick Slip.entries.1.name: Astute Slip
