Quick Slip.name: Quick Slip
Quick Toss.entries.0: As a bonus action, you can expend one superiority die and make a ranged attack with a weapon that has the thrown property. You can draw the weapon as part of making this attack. If you hit, add the superiority die to the weapon's damage roll.
Quick Toss.name: Quick Toss
Quickened Spell.entries.0: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to change the casting time to a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} for this casting. You can't modify a spell in this way if you've already cast a level 1+ spell on the current turn, nor can you cast a level 1+ spell on this turn after modifying a spell in this way.
Quickened Spell.name: Quickened Spell
Radiant Weapon.entries.0: This magic weapon grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. While holding it, the wielder can take a bonus action to cause it to shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. The wielder can extinguish the light as a bonus action.
Radiant Weapon.entries.1: The weapon has 4 charges. As a reaction immediately after being hit by an attack, the wielder can expend 1 charge and cause the attacker to be {@condition blinded} until the end of the attacker's next turn, unless the attacker succeeds on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. The weapon regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.
Radiant Weapon.name: Radiant Weapon
Rally.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one Superiority Die to bolster the resolve of a companion. Choose an ally of yours within 30 feet of yourself who can see or hear you. That creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Superiority Die roll plus half your Fighter level (round down).
Rally.name: Rally
Ranger.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast this spell using a 5th-level spell slot without expending a spell slot.
Ranger.entries.1: Once you cast the spell using this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Ranger.name: Следопыт
Rapid Regeneration.entries.1: At the start of each of your turns, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} regained).
Rapid Regeneration.name: Rapid Regeneration
Reach Attack.entries.0: As you hurl yourself into battle, your foes discover that trying to keep away from you won't save them. Your reach increases by 5 feet. This extra reach doesn't apply to {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.
Reach Attack.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, your extra reach from Reach Attack applies to {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.
Reach Attack.entries.1.name: Opportune Reach
Reach Attack.name: Reach Attack
Rebuke of the Talisman.entries.0: When the wearer of your talisman is hit by an attacker you can see within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to deal psychic damage to the attacker equal to your proficiency bonus and push it up to 10 feet away from the talisman's wearer.
Rebuke of the Talisman.name: Rebuke of the Talisman
Recognized in House.entries.0: When you request a favor from your house (as decribed under "{@optfeature Favored in House|EFA}"), it can take one of the following additional forms:
Recognized in House.entries.1.items.0: A dragonmarked member of the house casts for you a level 2 spell associated with the house's mark at no cost.
Recognized in House.entries.1.items.1: A member of the house casts for you a different spell of level 4 or lower at half the usual cost.
Recognized in House.entries.1.items.2: Whenever you take a Bastion turn, you can issue an order to your house as if it were a special facility in your Bastion. Your house determines the type of special facility available to you, as noted in each house's description later in this chapter.
Recognized in House.entries.2: In addition, your house stipend increases to support your Comfortable lifestyle (2 GP per day).
Recognized in House.name: Recognized in House
Reduce.entries.1: Your size decreases by one category (from Medium to Small, for example). You can spend an additional 2 Mutation Points to decrease your size by two categories instead (from Medium to Tiny, for example). See the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell to determine the benefits gained from this ability.
Reduce.name: Reduce
Regeneration (Armor Modification).entrie: You reinforce the armor with troll hide or soak it in vampire blood. You have a pool of six d10s. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a die from the pool, roll that die and add your Constitution modifier, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total.
You regain all the expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Regeneration (Armor Modification).name: Regeneration (Armor Modification)
Regeneration (Beast, Giant, Monstrosity): You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Monster Hunter level.
Regeneration.entries.0: The vampiric blood that flows in your veins gives you regenerative powers. You regain 15 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of your turn if you have at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} but less than 60 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. This feature has no effect if you are in sunlight or have taken Radiant damage since the end of your last turn.
Regeneration.name: Regeneration
Relentless Endurance.entries.0: The battles you need yet to fight are many, and death is not an option. When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Relentless Endurance.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Relentless Endurance, you drop to {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. Additionally, when you use Relentless Endurance, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to spend up to five {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice}, rolling them and gaining that number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Relentless Endurance.entries.1.name: Unparalleled Endurance
Relentless Endurance.name: Relentless Endurance
Relentless Hex.entries.0: Your curse creates a temporary bond between you and your target. As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of the target cursed by your {@spell hex} spell or by a warlock feature of yours, such as Hexblade's Curse or Sign of Ill Omen. To teleport in this way, you must be able to see the cursed target.
Relentless Hex.name: Relentless Hex
Repelling Blast.entries.0: Choose one of your known {@filter Warlock cantrips|spells|level=0|class=Warlock|spell attack=m;r;o} that requires an attack roll. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with that cantrip, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from you.
Repelling Blast.entries.1.entries.0.entr: You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different eligible cantrip.
Repelling Blast.entries.1.entries.0.name: Повторяемое
Repelling Blast.name: Repelling Blast
Replicate Magic Item.entries.0: Using this infusion, you replicate a particular magic item. You can learn this infusion multiple times; each time you do so, choose a magic item that you can make with it, picking from the Replicable Items tables below. A table's title tells you the level you must be in the class to choose an item from the table. Alternatively, you can choose the magic item from among the {@filter common magic items|items|type=!potion;!scroll|rarity=common} in the game, not including potions or scrolls.
Replicate Magic Item.entries.1: In the tables, an item's entry tells you whether the item requires attunement. See the item's description in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG} for more information about it, including the type of object required for its making.
Replicate Magic Item.entries.2.caption: Replicable Items (2nd-Level Artificer)
Replicate Magic Item.entries.3.caption: Replicable Items (6th-Level Artificer)
Replicate Magic Item.entries.4.caption: Replicable Items (10th-Level Artificer)
Replicate Magic Item.entries.5.caption: Replicable Items (14th-Level Artificer)
Replicate Magic Item.name: Replicate Magic Item
Resilient Ears.entries.0: Even as destruction rains down around you, your hearing stays sharp. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against having the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition.
Resilient Ears.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Perception|XPHB} checks involving hearing. Additionally, when you fail a saving throw against being {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Resilient Ears.entries.1.name: Determined Hearing
Resilient Ears.name: Resilient Ears
Resistance (Construct, Elemental, Undead: For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
Resistant Armor.entries.0: While wearing this armor, a creature has resistance to one of the following damage types, which you choose when you infuse the item: acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, radiant, or thunder.
Resistant Armor.name: Resistant Armor
Ride the Wind.entries.0: You can spend 4 ki points to cast {@spell fly}, targeting yourself.
Ride the Wind.name: Ride the Wind
Riposte.entries.0: When a creature misses you with a melee attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend one Superiority Die to make a melee attack roll with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against the creature. If you hit, add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage.
Riposte.name: Riposte
River of Hungry Flame.entries.0: You can spend 5 ki points to cast {@spell wall of fire}.
River of Hungry Flame.name: River of Hungry Flame
Rogue.entries.0: You've adopted the quick thinking of heroes who have outsmarted their foes. On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}: {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Hide|XPHB}.
Rogue.name: Плут
Roiling Elements.entries.0: Your physical vessel strains to contain the roiling elements trapped inside of you. In a moment of distraction your body may erupt momentarily to reveal your Primordial nature. Tongues of flame may lash out or arcs of lightning might burst from beneath your skin.
Roiling Elements.entries.1: You can contain the elements and maintain the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. Moments of stress are likely to unleash your true nature, which might occur under the following circumstances:
Roiling Elements.entries.2.items.3: Having the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions
Roiling Elements.entries.3: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, the GM can call for a Constitution {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB} with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage:
Roiling Elements.entries.4.caption: Rolling Elements Save DC
Roiling Elements.entries.5: When your Primordial nature is revealed, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on every {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} you make for 1 minute as you fight to control the volatile elements within you.
Roiling Elements.name: Roiling Elements
Runic Bomb.entries.0: Runic Bombs are used to hunt creatures resilient to mundane weapons. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can throw the runic bomb at a point  within 60 feet that you can see, creating a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point. The {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} spreads around corners, and its area is {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}. It lasts for 1 minute or until a strong wind (such as one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it. Whenever Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage is dealt to a creature inside the sphere, that damage is Force damage instead of its normal damage type.
Runic Bomb.entries.1: A Runic Bomb is destroyed after a single use.
Runic Bomb.name: Runic Bomb
Rush of the Gale Spirits.entries.0: You can spend 2 ki points to cast {@spell gust of wind}.
Rush of the Gale Spirits.name: Rush of the Gale Spirits
Ruthless Response.entries.0: A creature that gets the drop on you is met with a swift and brutal reply. When you take damage from a creature within your reach, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against that creature. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Ruthless Response.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls made using Ruthless Response.
Ruthless Response.entries.1.name: Focused Ruthlessness
Ruthless Response.name: Ruthless Response
Sacred Retribution.entries.0: When an ally you can see within 30 feet takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to imbue them with holy zeal, allowing them to make one additional attack. On a hit, the target takes an additional {@damage 1d8} Radiant damage. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Sacred Retribution.name: Sacred Retribution
Sangromancy Specialist.entries.0: Your magic is tinged with the blood that you crave. You gain the following features:
Sangromancy Specialist.entries.1.items.0: You gain an extra {@dice 1d12} Sangromancy {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} per Vampire Transformation. You can use those dice on Sangromancy spells. You regain all spent Sangromancy Dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Sangromancy Specialist.entries.1.items.1: When you successfully restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} using {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24}, you regain one spent Sangromancy Die.
Sangromancy Specialist.entries.1.items.2: Any cantrip you cast that does Necrotic damage can be enhanced by your Sangromancy Dice. You can spend up to 2 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} (including your Sangromancy Dice) to deal extra Necrotic damage with those cantrips. You can decide to spend these {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} after you know if the cantrip affects the target(s).
Sangromancy Specialist.name: Sangromancy Specialist
Savage Instincts.entries.0: You have developed an unrelenting thirst for bloodshed and carnage. While in your hybrid form, if you hit a {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} creature with a Claw or Bite attack, the attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} Slashing damage.
Savage Instincts.name: Savage Instincts
Scorpion Anchor.entries.0: This weapon is intended to keep flying foes anchored to the ground or stop monsters from fleeing. The Scorpion Anchor can be fired from a Longbow, Shortbow, or Crossbow as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action. When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a Scorpion Anchor, it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. A creature {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by the Scorpion Anchor can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your Trapper Gadget save DC. If it succeeds, it is no longer {@condition Restrained|XPHB}.
Scorpion Anchor.entries.1: A Scorpion Anchor is destroyed after the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature escapes or dies.
Scorpion Anchor.name: Scorpion Anchor
Sculptor of Flesh.entries.0: You can cast {@spell polymorph} once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Sculptor of Flesh.name: Sculptor of Flesh
Seasonally Affected.entries.0: Your connection to the Realm of Fey has a stronger pull on you. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the Fey Court attempts to claim your soul as their own.
Seasonally Affected.entries.1: Additionally, you gain {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to a certain damage type based on the Fey Court that you are pledged to. You cannot benefit from {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage.
Seasonally Affected.entries.2.entries.0: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Necrotic damage.
Seasonally Affected.entries.2.name: Spring
Seasonally Affected.entries.3.entries.0: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Cold damage.
Seasonally Affected.entries.3.name: Summer
Seasonally Affected.entries.4.entries.0: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Radiant damage.
Seasonally Affected.entries.4.name: Autumn
Seasonally Affected.entries.5.entries.0: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Fire damage.
Seasonally Affected.entries.5.name: Winter
Seasonally Affected.name: Seasonally Affected
Seeking Arrow.entries.0: Using divination magic, you grant your arrow the ability to seek out a target. When you use this option, you don't make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, choose one creature you have seen in the past minute. The arrow flies toward that creature, moving around corners if necessary and ignoring {@quickref Cover||3||three-quarters cover} and {@quickref Cover||3||half cover}. If the target is within the weapon's range and there is a path large enough for the arrow to travel to the target, the target must make a Dexterity saving throw. Otherwise, the arrow disappears after traveling as far as it can. On a failed save, the target takes damage as if it were hit by the arrow, plus an extra {@damage 1d6} force damage, and you learn the target's current location. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage, and you don't learn its location.
Seeking Arrow.entries.1: The force damage increases to {@damage 2d6} when you reach 18th level in this class.
Seeking Arrow.name: Seeking Arrow
Seeking Spell.entries.0: If you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to reroll the {@dice d20}, and you must use the new roll.
Seeking Spell.entries.1: You can use Seeking Spell even if you've already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.
Seeking Spell.name: Seeking Spell
Seraph Corruption.entries.0: The weight of evil in the world encumbers you more and more heavily as the days pass. Sometimes it becomes too much, and your celestial form is wracked with pain and doubt.
Seraph Corruption.entries.1: Whenever you roll a natural 1 on a saving throw, you suffer the following effects for 1 minute:
Seraph Corruption.entries.2.items.0: At the start of each of your turns, you take {@damage 1d10} Psychic damage. This damage cannot be reduced in any way.
Seraph Corruption.entries.2.items.1: You cannot regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or have {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.
Seraph Corruption.entries.2.items.2: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first attack you make every round.
Seraph Corruption.entries.2.items.3: Once per round, the first creature you force to make a saving throw against your spell has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.
Seraph Corruption.name: Seraph Corruption
Servant of the Autumn Court.entries.0: The Autumn Court embodies the careful scheming and approaching rot of the harvesting season. The Fey creatures of Autumn tend to be devious, scheming, and full of secrets. This is the court most associated with treants and other plant fey. Creatures of the Autumn Court often appear beautiful and helpful, but in truth their forms are hideous and methods vile.
Servant of the Autumn Court.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. One creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your starting or ending space takes {@damage 1d6} Poison damage. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Servant of the Autumn Court.name: Servant of the Autumn Court
Servant of the Spring Court.entries.0: The Spring Court embodies carnal desires, rebirth, and the planting and sowing season. The Fey creatures of Spring tend to be capricious, unreliable, and quick to flare their emotions. This is the court most associated with satyrs and nymphs. Creatures of the Spring Court hide savage intentions and uncontrollable urges behind gentle words.
Servant of the Spring Court.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. One creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your starting or ending space takes {@damage 1d6} Thunder damage. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Servant of the Spring Court.name: Servant of the Spring Court
Servant of the Summer Court.entries.0: The Summer Court embodies the lush warmth and manic celebration of the growing season. The Fey creatures of Summer tend toward joviality, excess, and vanity. This is the court most associated with dryads and pixies. Creatures of the Summer Court often hide their narcissism and cruelty behind beautiful façades.
Servant of the Summer Court.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. One creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your starting or ending space takes {@damage 1d6} Fire damage. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Servant of the Summer Court.name: Servant of the Summer Court
Servant of the Winter Court.entries.0: The Winter Court embodies the stillness, darkness, and death of the coldest season. The Fey creatures of Winter tend to be serious, cruel, and reflective. Creatures of the Winter Court are often terrifying to behold and very direct about their intentions.
Servant of the Winter Court.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. One creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your starting or ending space takes {@damage 1d6} Cold damage. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Servant of the Winter Court.name: Servant of the Winter Court
Shadow Arrow.entries.0: You weave illusion magic into your arrow, causing it to occlude your foe's vision with shadows. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra {@damage 2d6} psychic damage, and it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to see anything farther than 5 feet away until the start of your next turn.
Shadow Arrow.entries.1: The psychic damage increases to {@damage 4d6} when you reach 18th level in this class.
Shadow Arrow.name: Shadow Arrow
Shadowsteel Absorption.entries.0: As the Shadowsteel courses through you, it begins to harden your flesh. Your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} increases by 1 when you aren't wearing armor.
Shadowsteel Absorption.name: Shadowsteel Absorption
Shadowsteel Arcane Vessel.entries.0: When you cast a spell using a spell slot, the power of your Shadowsteel infection emanates from you. One creature targeted by the spell within 60 feet of you has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw to resist the spell, or you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the spell attack roll.
Shadowsteel Arcane Vessel.entries.1: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Shadowsteel Arcane Vessel.name: Shadowsteel Arcane Vessel
Shadowsteel Caster.entries.0: If you do not already have it, you gain the {@feat Shadowsteel Master|GrimHollowPG24} feat.
Shadowsteel Caster.entries.1: Additionally, when you use a Shadowsteel component to cast a spell using a spell slot, you can spend a Hit Die to regain that spell slot. The spell slot level cannot be greater than twice your Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation Stage.
Shadowsteel Caster.entries.2: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your spellcasting ability modifier. You regain all uses of this feature after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Shadowsteel Caster.name: Shadowsteel Caster
Shadowsteel Curser.entries.0: If you do not already have it, you gain the {@feat Shadowsteel Adept|GrimHollowPG24} feat.
Shadowsteel Curser.entries.1: Additionally, when you cast a Shadowsteel curse, the casting time is reduced by half, and the saving throw DC increases by 1.
Shadowsteel Curser.name: Shadowsteel Curser
Shadowsteel Explosion.entries.0: The Shadowsteel that suffuses your flesh responds to stress, and you can do nothing to control it. The first time you are {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} or reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} after you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you risk hurting everyone around you.
Shadowsteel Explosion.entries.1: When these events occur, all living creatures within 60 feet of you must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, creatures in the area suffer {@damage 4d10} Force damage and have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half damage and is not {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.
Shadowsteel Explosion.name: Shadowsteel Explosion
Shadowsteel Fury.entries.0: When you use the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to attack with your {@optfeature Shadowsteel Weapon|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel weapon} or {@optfeature Cursed Claw|GrimHollowPG24}, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make an additional attack with the same weapon or Claw. This extra attack carries with it the power of the Shadowsteel infection.
Shadowsteel Fury.entries.1: When you hit with this attack, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or gain a 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. The saving throw DC is 8 plus the ability modifier used in the attack plus your Transformation Stage.
Shadowsteel Fury.name: Shadowsteel Fury
Shadowsteel Weapon Master.entries.0: You gain a +4 bonus to damage rolls made with your {@optfeature Shadowsteel Weapon|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel weapon}.
Shadowsteel Weapon Master.name: Shadowsteel Weapon Master
Shadowsteel Weapon.entries.0: During a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you focus upon a Melee weapon within your reach, imbuing it with a fragment of your Shadowsteel corruption. This Shadowsteel weapon is considered magical.
Shadowsteel Weapon.entries.1: When you reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with a melee attack made with your Shadowsteel weapon, you gain {@dice 1d8} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} for 1 hour.
Shadowsteel Weapon.entries.2: You can repeat the process described above to imbue a different weapon with Shadowsteel. This removes the effect from your existing Shadowsteel weapon.
Shadowsteel Weapon.name: Shadowsteel Weapon
Shape the Flowing River.entries.0: As an action, you can spend 1 ki point to choose an area of ice or water no larger than 30 feet on a side within 120 feet of you. You can change water to ice within the area and vice versa, and you can reshape ice in the area in any manner you choose. You can raise or lower the ice's elevation, create or fill in a trench, erect or flatten a wall, or form a pillar. The extent of any such changes can't exceed half the area's largest dimension. For example, if you affect a 30-foot square, you can create a pillar up to 15 feet high. raise or lower the square's elevation by up to 15 feet, dig a trench up to 15 feet deep, and so on. You can't shape the ice to trap or injure a creature in the area.
Shape the Flowing River.name: Shape the Flowing River
Shapeshifter's Savagery.entries.0: You have embraced the animalistic side of your transformation. While in your hybrid form, you gain the following benefits:
Your Claw and Bite attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d8} Slashing damage.
You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions.
Shapeshifter's Savagery.name: Shapeshifter's Savagery
Shroud of Shadow.entries.0: You can cast {@spell invisibility} at will, without expending a spell slot.
Shroud of Shadow.name: Shroud of Shadow
Shroud of the Wild.entries.0: With any degree of obscuration, your instinctive ability to conceal yourself lets you avoid your enemies' notice. You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action even when you are only {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB} by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.
Shroud of the Wild.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Stealth|XPHB} checks using the {@action Hide|XPHB} action while using Shroud of the Wild.
Shroud of the Wild.entries.1.name: Faultless Shroud
Shroud of the Wild.name: Shroud of the Wild
Sign of Ill Omen.entries.0: You can cast {@spell bestow curse} once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Sign of Ill Omen.name: Sign of Ill Omen
Silver Sensitivity.entries.0: You have developed a debilitating sensitivity to silver. In all forms, you have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage from silvered weapons. In addition, you cannot have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to damage inflicted by silvered weapons.
Silver Sensitivity.name: Silver Sensitivity
Skill Prowess.entries.0: Your ingenuity and inventiveness help keep you alive in a dangerous world. Before you make an ability check using a skill you are proficient with, you can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} again. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Skill Prowess.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail an ability check made using the {@variantrule Skill|XPHB} Prowess trait, you can reroll the check and must use the new roll.
Skill Prowess.entries.1.name: Skill Mastery
Skill Prowess.name: Skill Prowess
Skin of Your Teeth.entries.0: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend one {@classFeature Risk|Gunslinger|ValdaGunslinger|2|ValdaGunslinger|Risk Die} to dodge out of harm's way. Roll the die and add the number rolled to your AC against this attack, potentially causing it to miss.
Skin of Your Teeth.name: Skin of Your Teeth
Skirmish Tactics.entries.0: Your brutal strike leaves your foe reeling as you slip away. When you hit a hostile creature with an attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} against you by that creature have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} until the end of your turn.
Skirmish Tactics.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you hit a hostile creature with an attack with a weapon attack or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} until the end of your turn.
Skirmish Tactics.entries.1.name: Supreme Skirmisher
Skirmish Tactics.name: Skirmish Tactics
Slimy Mien.entries.0: While manifesting your Ooze Form, you gain the following benefits:
Slimy Mien.entries.1.items.0: You are {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition.
Slimy Mien.entries.1.items.1: Your {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} range increases by 30 feet.
Slimy Mien.entries.1.items.2: You can communicate with Oozes within 30 feet as if you shared a language. Oozes are not automatically {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you, but they do not regard you as food and may respond to simple questions if they are not {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile}.
Slimy Mien.name: Slimy Mien
Slippery Ego.entries.0: As your brain becomes just one more part of the gooey mass that is you, and your sense of self becomes ever more tentative, the slightest distraction can cause you to forget who you were.
Slippery Ego.entries.1: The first time you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} after a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, or whenever you roll a 1 on a saving throw, the pain or surprise causes you lose your grip on your mind. You can't cast spells, activate magic items, understand language, or communicate in any intelligible way. You are overwhelmed with hunger and take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your next turn to make melee attacks against a random creature within reach. If there are no creatures within reach, you move until you are adjacent to the nearest creature, using the {@action Dash|XPHB} action if necessary.
Slippery Ego.entries.2: You may attempt a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of your turns to regain your sense of self. This effect ends automatically when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Slippery Ego.name: Slippery Ego
Slippery.entries.0: Any enemy that tries to grab you is in for a surprise. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Athletics|XPHB} and {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} checks to escape a grapple.
Slippery.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail an {@skill Athletics|XPHB} or {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} check to escape a grapple, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Slippery.entries.1.name: Supreme Slip
Slippery.name: Slippery
Sluggish.entries.0: Your body frequently shifts and melts without control, making you sluggish. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} decreases by 5 feet for every Transformation Stage you achieve.
Sluggish.entries.1: In addition, your skin glistens with oozy droplets leaving a trail anywhere you go. Ability checks made to follow your tracks are made with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
Sluggish.name: Sluggish
Soman Bloodline.entries.0: Your vampirism follows the Soman bloodline, the most prevalent in Etharis. You gain the following features:
Soman Bloodline.entries.1.items.0: Your Strength and Dexterity scores increase by 1. Neither score can exceed 19 with this feature.
Soman Bloodline.entries.1.items.1: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls with {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24}.
Soman Bloodline.entries.1.items.2: The Necrotic damage dealt by {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} increases to {@dice 1d8}.
Soman Bloodline.entries.1.items.3: You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Soman Bloodline.name: Soman Bloodline
Sorcerer.entries.0: Choose one of the following Metamagic Options available to the Sorcerer class: {@optfeature Careful Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Distant Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Empowered Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Extended Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Seeking Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Subtle Spell|XPHB}, or {@optfeature Transmuted Spell|XPHB}.
Sorcerer.entries.1: When you cast a Bard spell, you can apply that Metamagic Option as if you had paid its Sorcery Point cost.
Sorcerer.entries.2: Once you use this talent, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Sorcerer.name: Чародей
Soul Vessel.entries.0: You have successfully torn your soul from your body and trapped it in a suitable object. The object must be a trinket or item no larger than 1 cubic foot in size. This item becomes your soul vessel.
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.0: A soul vessel is an enchanted vessel containing a Lich's soul. Additionally, a soul vessel is a conduit for the lich to feed on captured souls. A soul vessel has the following statistics:
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.1.items.1: {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}: 10 x your Character Level
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.1.items.3: {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Damage Immunities}: Poison, Psychic; Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing from non-magical sources
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.1.items.4: {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Damage Resistances}: Cold, Fire, Lightning, Necrotic, Thunder; Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing from magical sources
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.2: While a soul is stored in your soul vessel, the soul vessel is considered charged. The soul contained within the soul vessel must be from a creature whose {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} is equal to or higher than half your Character Level. The soul vessel can only contain one soul at a time.
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.3: You can add a soul to the soul vessel 2 times for each of your Transformation Stages. You regain this ability when you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.4: To store a soul within the soul vessel, the lich must use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action within 1 minute of a creature's death, or a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} triggered by the creature's death, while holding the vessel to capture the soul, and the dead creature'sbody must be within 60 feet of the vessel when the capture occurs. The creature cannot be returned to life until its soul is removed from your soul vessel.
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.5: If your soul vessel is reduced to 0 hit points, you must create a new one. Doing so takes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Until you do, you do not gain any of the benefits of having the soul vessel. Also, until you have a soul vessel, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} you make after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Soul Vessel.entries.1.entries.6: If you are killed while you control a charged soul vessel on the same plane of existence, your soul vessel's charge is consumed. You are brought back to life as per the {@spell Resurrection|XPHB} spell within 5 feet of your soul vessel 1 day later. If you are killed and your soul vessel is not charged, you are resurrected 7 days later instead.
Soul Vessel.entries.1.name: Soul Vessel
Soul Vessel.name: Soul Vessel
Soul-Shattering Attack.entries.0: After you make a weapon attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can spend the charge in your soul vessel to add the {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} of the soul within it to the attack roll and damage dealt with that attack. If the creature dies from that attack, you can instantly recharge your soul vessel with the soul that you just expended.
Soul-Shattering Attack.entries.1: You can use this feature a number of times equal to 4 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Soul-Shattering Attack.name: Soul-Shattering Attack
Spectral Form.entries.0: You are considered to be Undead in addition to your existing creature type(s). You cannot be excluded from Turn Undead.
Spectral Form.entries.1: Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. If you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage from another source, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} instead.
Spectral Form.entries.2: Finally, you stop aging. You are immune to any effect that would age you, and you cannot die from old age.
Spectral Form.name: Spectral Form
Spellshine Ointment.entries.0: For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.
Spellshine Ointment.name: Spellshine Ointment
Stalwart Reserves.entries.0: Each time you lay into a foe, their state of peril lends you vigor. When you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll a number of d4s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the total rolled. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Stalwart Reserves.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can take the maximum number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} rather than rolling.
Stalwart Reserves.entries.1.name: Stalwart Edge
Stalwart Reserves.name: Stalwart Reserves
Standing Leap.entries.0: Threats on the ground are of little concern as you leap over them with ease. You can make a {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} of up to 20 feet and a {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} of up to 10 feet, with or without a running start. If your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is less than the distance you can {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB}, you can leap only a distance equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Standing Leap.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can make a {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} of up to 30 feet and a {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} of up to 15 feet, as limited by your speed.
Standing Leap.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, when you jump out of another creature's reach, the movement of the jump does not provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} from that creature. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Standing Leap.entries.1.name: Incredible Leap
Standing Leap.name: Standing Leap
Steady.entries.0: No matter what kind of upheaval surrounds you, you stand your ground. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against having the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
Steady.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, standing from {@condition Prone|XPHB} takes only five feet of movement instead of half your movement.
Steady.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, when you fail a saving throw against being knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Steady.entries.1.name: Stand Fast
Steady.name: Steady
Stealthy (Armor Modification).entries.0: Your armor is draped with a shadowy cloak or made from hide as light as a feather. Your armor doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks, even if it would normally. While wearing your armor, creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks to see you, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks to notice creatures.
Stealthy (Armor Modification).name: Stealthy (Armor Modification)
Steelskin Ointment.entries.0: For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
Steelskin Ointment.name: Steelskin Ointment
Stone Rune.entries.0: This rune's magic channels the judiciousness associated with stone giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks, and you have {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 120 feet.
Stone Rune.entries.1: In addition, when a creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to invoke the rune and force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. Unless the save succeeds, the creature is {@condition charmed} by you for 1 minute. While {@condition charmed} in this way, the creature has a speed of 0 and is {@condition incapacitated}, descending into a dreamy stupor. The creature repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Stone Rune.name: Stone Rune
Storm Rune.entries.0: Using this rune, you can glimpse the future like a storm giant seer. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) checks, and you can't be {@status surprised} as long as you aren't {@condition incapacitated}.
Storm Rune.entries.1: In addition, you can invoke the rune as a bonus action to enter a prophetic state for 1 minute or until you're {@condition incapacitated}. Until the state ends, when you or another creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, a saving throw, or an ability check, you can use your reaction to cause the roll to have advantage or disadvantage. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Storm Rune.name: Storm Rune
Strigoi Bloodline.entries.0: Your vampirism follows the Strigoi bloodline. Your speed and guile almost match your ferocity. You gain the following features:
Strigoi Bloodline.entries.1.items.0: Your Dexterity score increases by 2. Your Dexterity score cannot exceed 20 with this feature.
Strigoi Bloodline.entries.1.items.1: If you make your {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}, the Piercing damage increases to {@dice 2d4} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier.
Strigoi Bloodline.entries.1.items.2: You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and you can use the {@action Hide|XPHB} action anywhere that is not in sunlight.
Strigoi Bloodline.entries.1.items.3: You have proficiency with the {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skill. If you are already proficient in {@skill Stealth|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in Stealth instead.
Strigoi Bloodline.name: Strigoi Bloodline
Subtle Spell.entries.0: When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to cast it without any Verbal, Somatic, or Material components, except Material components that are consumed by the spell or that have a cost specified in the spell.
Subtle Spell.name: Subtle Spell
Superior Technique.entries.0: You learn one {@filter maneuver|optionalfeatures|feature type=MV:B} of your choice from among those available to the {@class fighter|phb|Battle Master|Battle Master|phb|2-0} archetype. If a maneuver you use requires your target to make a saving throw to resist the maneuver's effects, the saving throw DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).
Superior Technique.entries.1: You gain one superiority die, which is a {@dice d6} (this die is added to any superiority dice you have from another source). This die is used to fuel your maneuvers. A superiority die is expended when you use it. You regain your expended superiority dice when you finish a short or long rest.
Superior Technique.name: Superior Technique
Supernatural Hide.entries.1: У вас есть {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Сопротивление} Дробящему, Колющему и Рубящему урону.
Supernatural Hide.name: Supernatural Hide
Supple Squeeze.entries.0: With an effort of will, you contort your body into the tightest spaces. You can squeeze through a space that is large enough for a creature two sizes smaller than you, rather than one size smaller.
Supple Squeeze.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, squeezing does not cost you additional movement, and you do not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws while squeezing.
Supple Squeeze.entries.1.name: Full-Speed Squeeze
Supple Squeeze.name: Supple Squeeze
Supreme Sanguine Curse.entries.0: Your curse continues to transform you, bringing about further debilitations. You gain the following features:
Supreme Sanguine Curse.entries.1.items.0: If you start your turn in sunlight, you take {@damage 1d10} Radiant damage. You cannot have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} or {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to this Radiant Damage.
Supreme Sanguine Curse.entries.1.items.1: Your appearance begins to resemble that of a true vampire. In stressful conditions, as determined by you or the GM, your appearance changes outside of your control. When you do, anyone observing you knows your true nature.
Supreme Sanguine Curse.entries.2: You can hide your true appearance and disguise yourself as the humanoid you once were by {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on your composure. However, moments of bloodlust or stress are likely to reveal your true nature, including the following situations:
Supreme Sanguine Curse.entries.4: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call or a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage.  If you fail this save, your Sanguine Curse is revealed.
Supreme Sanguine Curse.entries.5.caption: Supreme Sanguine Curse Save DC
Supreme Sanguine Curse.entries.6: Non-evil creatures that witness your true form become instantly {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, unless the GM decides otherwise.
Supreme Sanguine Curse.name: Supreme Sanguine Curse
Sweeping Attack.entries.0: When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to damage another creature. Choose another creature within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to the number you roll on your Superiority Die. The damage is of the same type dealt by the original attack.
Sweeping Attack.name: Sweeping Attack
Sweeping Cinder Strike.entries.0: You can spend 2 ki points to cast {@spell burning hands}.
Sweeping Cinder Strike.name: Sweeping Cinder Strike
Swimmer.entries.0: You are in your element while in the water, moving with grace and ease. You have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Swimmer.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while swimming.
Swimmer.entries.1.name: Quickened Swim
Swimmer.name: Swimmer
Tactical Assessment.entries.0: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill History|XPHB} or {@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check or a Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check, you can expend one Superiority Die and add that die to the ability check.
Tactical Assessment.name: Tactical Assessment
Telepathy (Aberration, Fiend, Plant).ent: For 1 hour, you can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself. Your telepathic utterances are in a language you know, and the creature understands you only if it knows that language. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage.
Tenacious.entries.0: Your enemies might put you down, but you are never down for long. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}.
Tenacious.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but don't die outright, you remain conscious. You must make {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as normal while at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and you suffer a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure each time you take any damage, but you can otherwise act freely. You can't become {@variantrule Stable|XPHB} while you remain at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in this way.
Tenacious.entries.1.name: Трудноубиваемый
Tenacious.name: Tenacious
Tenmen Tincture.entries.0: For 1 hour, you count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity, and when you attack with a Melee weapon whose mastery property you can use, you can replace that property with the {@itemMastery Topple|XPHB} property for that attack.
Tenmen Tincture.name: Tenmen Tincture
Terrain Cloak.entries.0: Composed of local materials, a Terrain Cloak allows wearers to conceal themselves within the environment. Terrain Cloaks can be worn over Light or Medium armor and are donned and doffed with the speed of Light armor.
Terrain Cloak.entries.1: Creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks to see you.
Terrain Cloak.entries.2: The item lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, at which point the item falls apart.
Terrain Cloak.name: Terrain Cloak
Terrifying Visage.entries.0: Your mutations and unearthly appearance can unsettle even the bravest creatures. When you invoke your Aberrant Mutations or Writhing Tendrils, you choose one creature within 30 feet that can see you. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or gain the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The DC for the saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Terrifying Visage.entries.1: A creature that makes its saving throw is immune to this effect for 24 hours.
Terrifying Visage.name: Terrifying Visage
The Green Sisterhood.entries.0: Green Hags are skilled deceivers who collect corrupting magic and secret curses. They use their magic to twist pure into foul, poking at the weaknesses of their foes. Green Hags embrace magical deceptions and are known for their vile recipes. There are no worse cooks in Etharis.
The Green Sisterhood.entries.1.items.0: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet.
The Green Sisterhood.entries.1.items.1: You become Amphibious and can breathe both air and water.
The Green Sisterhood.entries.1.items.2: You can mimic animal sounds and humanoid voices.
The Green Sisterhood.entries.2: A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a {@action Study|XPHB} action and a successful Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check with a DC equal to 8 plus your Charisma modifier plus your Transformation Stage.
The Green Sisterhood.name: The Green Sisterhood
The Red Sisterhood.entries.0: Red Hags are sometimes called the "nice" hags due to their ability to pretend to be helpful, but their kindness is a mask. While they are more subtle than other Hags and may choose to do good deeds, their motives are selfish. They delight in using honeyed words and offering deals that are just too good to pass up.
The Red Sisterhood.entries.1.items.0: У вас есть {@sense Darkvision|XPHB|Тёмное зрение} дальностью 60 футов.
The Red Sisterhood.entries.1.items.1: You gain proficiency in {@skill Deception|XPHB} and {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}. If you are already proficient, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} instead.
The Red Sisterhood.entries.1.items.2: You are immutable. No effect can change your form, and an illusion can disguise you only if the illusion gives you the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition.
The Red Sisterhood.name: The Red Sisterhood
The Sanguine Curse.entries.0: The Sanguine Curse has taken hold of you. As a result, you gain the following features:
The Sanguine Curse.entries.1.items.0: You cannot enter a private residence that you do not own without one of the occupants inviting you. Once invited, you can enter without penalty as long as the inviter resides there. If you do enter uninvited, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} while in the residence. Additionally, you take {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage at the start of each of your turns. You cannot be {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistant} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to Psychic damage while inside such a residence uninvited.
The Sanguine Curse.entries.1.items.1: You must feed at least once within seven days of your last feeding. See the Feeding sidebar for more information.
The Sanguine Curse.entries.2.entries.0: По мере того как Вампир всё дальше отходит от своей смертной формы, меняются и его метаболические потребности. Первое изменение — потребность в крови смертных. Эта потребность составляет примерно 1 пинту крови Гуманоида, Зверя или иного существа со смертной кровью.
The Sanguine Curse.entries.2.entries.1: As an action, you can bite a living creature within 5 feet that is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you, or that has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. You drain the target of one pint of blood (or similar life-giving substance), leaving a visible bite mark on that creature. The bitten creature gains one {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.
The Sanguine Curse.entries.2.entries.2: A Vampire that does not feed within the required time enters a rabid feeding frenzy under the GM's control. They attack the nearest creature they could feed from. A Vampire remains in this state until they have drained a creature completely (killing them), at which point the Vampire falls {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} for 4 hours.
The Sanguine Curse.entries.2.name: Питание
The Sanguine Curse.name: The Sanguine Curse
The Sea Sisterhood.entries.0: Sea Hags enjoy spreading chaos. They hate order and calm, creating elaborate deceptions that lead to despair when uncovered. Their appearances are especially hideous, and their gaze can cause fear, or even kill. You gain the following benefits.
The Sea Sisterhood.entries.1.items.0: У вас есть {@sense Darkvision|XPHB|Тёмное зрение} дальностью 60 футов.
The Sea Sisterhood.entries.1.items.1: You become Amphibious and can breathe both air and water. You gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. If you already have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 10 feet.
The Sea Sisterhood.entries.1.items.2: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can gaze upon a creature you can see within 30 feet. If the creature fails a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 plus your Charisma modifier plus your Transformation Stage, it takes {@damage 1d8} Psychic damage for each Transformation Stage you have.
The Sea Sisterhood.name: The Sea Sisterhood
Thief of Five Fates.entries.0: You can cast {@spell bane} once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Thief of Five Fates.name: Thief of Five Fates
Thirsting Blade.entries.0: You gain the Extra Attack feature for your pact weapon only. With that feature, you can attack twice with the weapon instead of once when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.
Thirsting Blade.name: Thirsting Blade
Timely Boon.entries.0: Fortune favors you at times when a threat might send you down. When you fail a saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll a {@dice d4} and add it to the save, potentially turning it into a success. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Timely Boon.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you roll a {@dice d8} instead of a {@dice d4} when you use Timely Boon.
Timely Boon.entries.1.name: Born Lucky
Timely Boon.name: Timely Boon
Tireless.entries.0: An innate resilience lets you shake off conditions that would take others down. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws connected to gaining or removing {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels.
Tireless.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Tireless.entries.1.name: Vigorous
Tireless.name: Tireless
Tomb of Levistus.entries.0: As a reaction when you take damage, you can entomb yourself in ice, which melts away at the end of your next turn. You gain 10 temporary hit points per warlock level, which take as much of the triggering damage as possible. Immediately after you take the damage, you gain vulnerability to fire damage, your speed is reduced to 0, and you are {@condition incapacitated}. These effects, including any remaining temporary hit points, all end when the ice melts.
Tomb of Levistus.entries.1: Once you use this invocation, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Tomb of Levistus.name: Tomb of Levistus
Tooth and Claw.entries.0: You can manifest fangs, tusks, claws, or other means of harming your enemies. These manifestations also deliver a powerful magic jolt.
Tooth and Claw.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you manifest some natural defense mechanism for 1 minute. This gives you the following benefits:
Tooth and Claw.entries.2.items.0: You manifest a Claw, Bite, or Gore attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. This manifested attack deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Tooth and Claw.entries.2.items.1: After taking an {@action Attack|XPHB} action that did not use your manifested attack, you may take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later in that turn to use your manifested attack once.
Tooth and Claw.entries.2.items.2: Once per round, when you hit a target with a manifested attack, you can choose to deal an additional {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage to the target. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. The DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Tooth and Claw.entries.3: You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this ability after completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Tooth and Claw.name: Tooth and Claw
Touch of Life.entries.0: Effects that corrupt the essence of other living creatures are of little concern to you. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.
Touch of Life.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against an effect that deals Necrotic damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Touch of Life.entries.1.name: Strength of Life
Touch of Life.name: Touch of Life
Toughness.entries.0: An intrinsic hardiness marks you as one born for battle. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 each time you gain a level.
Toughness.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by 2 instead of 1, and it increases by 2 each time you gain a level.
Toughness.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, when you make a saving throw against an effect that would decrease your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the save. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Toughness.entries.1.name: Extra Tough
Toughness.name: Toughness
Transmuted Spell.entries.0: When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to change that damage type to one of the other listed types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Thunder.
Transmuted Spell.name: Transmuted Spell
Tricksters Escape.entries.0: You can cast {@spell freedom of movement} once on yourself without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Tricksters Escape.name: Trickster's Escape
Trip Attack.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend one Superiority Die and add the die to the attack's damage roll. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
Trip Attack.name: Trip Attack
True Name.entries.0: Your Fiend transformation is complete, and you are reborn. You are assigned a new name by the denizens of the Netherworld, which becomes your True Name. You receive a talisman of brimstone and brass with your true name inscribed in Infernal on it.
True Name.entries.1: A creature with a {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} of 10 or higher within 10 feet of you that knows your True Name can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and speak your True Name. You must succeed on a DC 20 Charisma saving throw or suffer the following effects for 1 minute. You may attempt a Charisma saving throw at the end of each of your turns to end the effects.
True Name.entries.2.items.0: You have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition, even if you are {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to being {@condition Charmed|XPHB}.
True Name.entries.2.items.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, the entity that has spoken your True Name can force you to move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and use your action to attack with a weapon, use your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or cast a cantrip against a target of their choice.
True Name.entries.2.items.2: All attacks against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws.
True Name.entries.2.items.3: You cannot regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or have {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. You immediately lose any {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} you have.
True Name.name: True Name
Twilight Glamour.entries.0: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you gain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for 1 hour or until you end it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. If you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell that causes the target to make a saving throw, the remaining duration changes to 1 minute.
Twilight Glamour.entries.1: You can use this ability twice, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Twilight Glamour.name: Twilight Glamour
Twinned Spell.entries.0: When you cast a spell, such as {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot to target an additional creature, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to increase the spell's effective level by 1.
Twinned Spell.name: Twinned Spell
Two-Faced.entries.0: You can transform your face into a vision of enchantment or horror that affects creatures around you, based on the Fey Court you serve. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you take on this visage. Creatures within 30 feet who can see you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw with a DC equal to 8 plus your Charisma modifier plus your Transformation Stage or be affected for 1 minute.
Two-Faced.entries.1: You can do this a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Two-Faced.entries.2.entries.0: Any creature that fails the saving throw has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition. The creature can attempt a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of their turns to end the condition. This effect ends early on a creature if you or an ally deal damage to it or take other harmful actions.
Two-Faced.entries.2.name: Spring
Two-Faced.entries.3.entries.0: Any creature that fails the saving throw gains the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition. This effect ends early on a creature if you or an ally deal damage to it or take other harmful actions.
Two-Faced.entries.3.name: Summer
Two-Faced.entries.4.entries.0: Any creature that fails the saving throw gains the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. The creature can attempt a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of their turns to end the condition.
Two-Faced.entries.4.name: Autumn
Two-Faced.entries.5.entries.0: Any creature that fails the saving throw becomes {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of you. This effect ends early if a creature affected by this ability ends its turn out of your line of sight.
Two-Faced.entries.5.name: Winter
Two-Faced.name: Two-Faced
Ultimate Brand.entries.0: When a creature affected by your {@optfeature Daemonic Brand|GrimHollowPG24} starts its turn, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to dictate its move and the action it takes. If you do, the creature repeats the initial Wisdom saving throw at the end of its turn. On a success, you can't use this {@action Reaction|XPHB} again while your brand lasts.
Ultimate Brand.name: Ultimate Brand
Ultimate Predator.entries.0: You realize the true cost of your transformation. The beast within has gained more control of your body than you have. While you can control it at times, you know it cannot be contained forever—and when it breaks free, it delights in any slaughter it can find.
Ultimate Predator.entries.2.items.0: If you can see, hear or smell a {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} creature, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws.
Ultimate Predator.entries.2.items.1: At the start of your turn, if you can see, hear or smell a creature with the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, you must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you transform into your hybrid form, or, if already in hybrid form you go feral as described under Lust for Hunt, above. On a success, you are immune to this effect until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Ultimate Predator.entries.2.items.2: Whenever you kill a creature while in your hybrid form, you cannot transform back into your normal form until dawn the next day.
Ultimate Predator.name: Ultimate Predator
Ultimate Sanguine Curse.entries.0: Your transformation into a creature of the night is complete. And with that transformation comes susceptibilities. You gain the following features:
One strike may end your existence forever. When you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}, and you are struck by a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with a Piercing Weapon made of wood or silver, you must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you die and are reduced to ash. Only a {@spell True Resurrection|XPHB} or {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell can resurrect you.
Ultimate Sanguine Curse.name: Ultimate Sanguine Curse
Unbroken Air.entries.0: You can create a blast of compressed air that strikes like a mighty fist. As an action, you can spend 2 ki points and choose a creature within 30 feet of you. That creature must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d10} bludgeoning damage, plus an extra {@damage 1d10} bludgeoning damage for each additional ki point you spend, and you can push the creature up to 20 feet away from you and knock it {@condition prone}. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage, and you don't push it or knock it {@condition prone}.
Unbroken Air.name: Unbroken Air
Unchecked.entries.0: Your ability to stay in motion is second to none, and foes try in vain to pin you down. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition Restrained|XPHB}.
Unchecked.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against being {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Unchecked.entries.1.name: Slip Free
Unchecked.name: Unchecked
Undead Form.entries.0: You become an Undead in addition to any other creature type(s). You cannot be excluded from Turn Undead.
Undead Form.entries.1: You also stop aging. You are immune to any effect that would age you, and you cannot die from old age. You do not require air, food, drink, or sleep.
Undead Form.name: Undead Form
Undead Resilience.entries.0: You become supernaturally tough to kill. When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by any type of damage except Radiant, you can choose to be reduced to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Undead Resilience.name: Undead Resilience
Undying Servitude.entries.0: You can cast {@spell animate dead} without using a spell slot. Once you do so, you can't cast it in this way again until you finish a long rest.
Undying Servitude.name: Undying Servitude
Unholy Healing.entries.0: Your magical connection to your soul vessel restores your vitality quickly. While your soul vessel is charged, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to draw healing power from it. At the start of each of your turns for 1 minute, you regain 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The soul vessel does not lose its charge.
Unholy Healing.entries.1: You can use this feature three times, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Unholy Healing.name: Unholy Healing
Unnatural Healer.entries.0: Your innate healing abilities let you recover from some of the grimmest wounds. During a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can automatically reverse Grievous Wounds. Additionally, you can reattach any severed body parts (fingers, legs, tails, and so on), which are automatically restored at the end of the {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If your severed body parts aren't available, you can replace them with the same body parts of another creature of the same general anatomy as you. If you wish to intentionally swap out body parts with replacements, you can sever your own body parts with no pain or discomfort.
Unnatural Healer.entries.1: The ability to make use of unusual body parts (for example, giving yourself the taloned paw of a lion if you lose a hand) are left to the GM's discretion. In any event, swapping a severed body part for an unusual body part grants you no mechanical Advantages not covered by other traits (see "Features and Traits" on page 11).
Unnatural Healer.entries.2.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you automatically reverse Permanent Wounds during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Additionally, you can restore any severed body part during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, as if subject to the {@spell Regenerate|XPHB} spell. You can use this trait to create unusual regenerated body parts at the GM's determination.
Unnatural Healer.entries.2.name: Regenerative Healer
Unnatural Healer.name: Unnatural Healer
Unstable Existence.entries.0: Magic causes your physical form to unravel. Whenever you roll a natural 1 or 2 on a saving throw against a spell, you reveal your {@optfeature Hideous Appearance (Aberrant Horror)|GrimHollowPG24|Hideous Appearance} and must roll again on the {@table Unstable Form|GrimHollowPG24|Unstable Form table}. If the result is less than your current Unstable Form effect, replace it with the new result.
Unstable Existence.name: Unstable Existence
Unstable Form.entries.0: Your body becomes malleable and struggles to maintain any one physical shape. Upon completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} after an adventuring day in which you took damage, you must roll {@dice 1d100} on the {@table Unstable Form|GrimHollowPG24|Unstable Form table} and apply the corresponding mutation based on current your Transformation Stage. This mutation lasts until you finish another {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Unstable Form.name: Unstable Form
Untethered from Life.entries.0: Your life force is weaker than other creatures. When you would regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you regain half as many {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as normal (rounded down).
Untethered from Life.name: Untethered from Life
Venomous Attacks.entries.1: Once per turn, a creature you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Poison damage, and you can force a creature you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack to make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 minute. It can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on a success.
Venomous Attacks.entries.2.entries.0: You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times. For each additional 2 Mutation points, the attack does an additional {@damage 2d6} Poison damage.
Venomous Attacks.entries.2.name: Повторяемое
Venomous Attacks.name: Venomous Attacks
Viscous Durability.entries.0: Your mind and body become more Ooze-like. You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and Acid damage.
Viscous Durability.entries.1: When you gain this Boon, choose Cold, Fire, or Lightning; you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the chosen damage type.
Viscous Durability.name: Viscous Durability
Visions of Distant Realms.entries.0: You can cast {@spell Arcane Eye|XPHB} without expending a spell slot.
Visions of Distant Realms.name: Visions of Distant Realms
Voice of the Chain Master.entries.0: You can communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar's senses as long as you are on the same plane of existence. Additionally, while perceiving through your familiar's senses, you can also speak through your familiar in your own voice, even if your familiar is normally incapable of speech.
Voice of the Chain Master.name: Voice of the Chain Master
Wall Crawler.entries.1: You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your speed. For an additional 1 Mutation Point, you gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to twice your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Wall Crawler.name: Wall Crawler
Warlock.entries.0: You learn one {@filter Eldritch Invocation|optionalfeatures|feature type=EI} of your choice. If an invocation has a level requirement, use your Bard level.
Warlock.entries.1: When you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the invocations you know and replace it with another invocation for which you qualify.
Warlock.name: Колдун
Water Whip.entries.0: You can spend 2 ki points as an action to create a whip of water that shoves and pulls a creature to unbalance it. A creature that you can see that is within 30 feet of you must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d10} bludgeoning damage, plus an extra {@damage 1d10} bludgeoning damage for each additional ki point you spend, and you can either knock it {@condition prone} or pull it up to 25 feet closer to you. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage, and you don't pull it or knock it {@condition prone}.
Water Whip.name: Water Whip
Wave of Rolling Earth.entries.0: You can spend 6 ki points to cast {@spell wall of stone}.
Wave of Rolling Earth.name: Wave of Rolling Earth
Weakened Constitution.entries.0: Your connection to the Fey Courts has granted you tremendous power but also an inherent fragility. When you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} for the first time after you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you gain a level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}.
